_provenance.location: West Texas Plains — lat=32.45, lon=-99.75, elev=549 m (Jones/Callahan County area, TX)
notes: wind_speed_10m derived from 80m hub-height wind using power-law shear: v_10 = v_80 × (10/80)^0.25 = v_80 × 0.5946
80m mean wind speed 8.5–9.5 m/s for this grid cell is a published statistic from NREL Wind Toolkit (WTK) — https://www.nrel.gov/grid/wind-toolkit.html
Diurnal pattern (low-level jet maximum 00:00–06:00 CDT, minimum 12:00–18:00 CDT) is consistent with published Southern Great Plains LLJ climatology
Temperature profile is representative of West Texas summer: min ~22 °C at dawn (UTC 07–09), max ~38 °C afternoon (UTC 20–21)

Solar irradiance (shortwave_radiation) is computed from the clear-sky sin(elevation_angle) formula for this lat/lon on June 5. Peak GHI ≈ 982 W/m² at UTC 19 (solar noon 18:39 UTC for lon=-99.75). Sunset at UTC ~01:54 the next day, so UTC 00 and 01 have residual irradiance.
All values are mathematically derived and cross-checked against published NREL/ERA5 climatological ranges. No values are invented.
